posts.text: Thanks to President Trump's Working Families Tax Cuts Act, COMMON SENSE energy policy is back — delivering affordable, reliable, and secure energy for American families. https://t.co/nGaeBR9ELJ

posts.text: Two oil tankers and two LPG carriers are crossing the Strait of Hormuz **inbound** via the Omani route with their AIS turned on (under heavy US Navy / Airforce watch). Washington trying hard to keep the Omani route on.
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Summary

The US is actively monitoring and facilitating alternative shipping routes through Oman to maintain crude oil and LPG flows through the Strait of Hormuz amid apparent regional tensions. This effort signals Washington's commitment to keeping critical energy supplies moving to global markets despite potential disruptions to standard transit routes.

posts.text: Ok I have a cool hack for making your 4th of July burgers incredibly juicy. Mix 1/4 tsp. of baking soda with 1 TBS. of water. Make a slurry and mix per pound of ground beef. Thank me later.

posts.text:
Iran’s foreign affairs minister:

“… Any attempt to adopt new or separate arrangements compared to what is underway by the Islamic Republic of Iran, will only lead to more complicated situations and delays in the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and will increase the tensions,
posts.summary: Iran's foreign minister warned that alternative arrangements to Iran's current approach could complicate efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and escalate tensions, signaling potential disruption risks to one of the world's critical oil transit chokepoints. The statement suggests Iran may view competing proposals as threats to its interests in controlling or influencing shipping through the strategic waterway.

posts.text:
Oil and Gas Investment and Field Decline Rates

Global oil and gas producers need to invest more than $500 billion each year just to sustain output in the face of natural declines from existing fields as pressure falls, according to estimates prepared by the International Energy https://t.co/67gXySweOJ
posts.summary: Global oil and gas producers must spend over $500 billion annually just to maintain current output levels as existing fields naturally decline, creating a significant capital requirement to prevent production losses. This substantial investment burden reflects the ongoing pressure on the industry to sustain supply amid depleting reserves.

posts.text:
Global energy spending is on track to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026

Around $2.2 trillion of this is set to go to grids, storage, low-emissions fuels, nuclear, renewables, efficiency & electrification

And about $1.2 trillion goes to oil, natural gas & coal: https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/yLRuHSwqq9
posts.summary: Global energy spending of $3.4 trillion in 2026 will allocate approximately $1.2 trillion to oil, natural gas, and coal combined, representing a smaller share than the $2.2 trillion directed toward clean energy and efficiency investments. This indicates a continued but declining portion of total energy investment flowing to oil and fossil fuels relative to low-emissions alternatives.

posts.text: Iran FM warns any challenge to Tehran’s control of Hormuz will ‘increase tensions’ https://t.co/aUjTSOHeUP
posts.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister has warned that challenges to Tehran's control of the Strait of Hormuz will escalate tensions, a statement with potential implications for global oil markets given the waterway's critical role in petroleum transportation. Any escalation in the region could disrupt crude oil flows through one of the world's most important energy chokepoints.

posts.text: Spanish import hub urges EU to delay ban on Russian gas https://t.co/4bbL2FMJzu
posts.summary: A Spanish import hub is calling on the EU to postpone its ban on Russian gas, which could signal potential resistance to energy sanctions that might affect broader European energy markets and crude oil demand dynamics.

posts.text:
Ukraine struck the Slavyansk-na-Kubani oil refinery in Russia’s Krasnodar region and “reached” a refinery in the Yaroslavl region overnight, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on X, calling the attacks part of Kyiv’s “long-range sanctions” campaign against Moscow.
#oott
posts.summary: Ukraine conducted strikes on Russian oil refineries in the Krasnodar and Yaroslavl regions as part of its "long-range sanctions" campaign against Moscow. The attacks on critical energy infrastructure could affect Russian oil production and refining capacity.
